# Asaptic Cross-Standard — full corpus map (llms-full.txt)
# Public-interest, source-linked China-export compliance comparisons. AI-compiled, multi-model cross-verified, NOT human-verified; informational only — verify with the official regulator. Trilingual (en/zh-Hans/zh-Hant). License CC-BY-4.0.
# Updated: 2026-06-14 | 174 comparisons · 23 product categories · 22 markets
# Machine-readable index: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/index.json

## Battery energy storage (BESS)
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): RCM / EESS registration, AS/NZS IEC 62619 cell-safety testing at an ILAC-accredited lab, AS/NZS 5139:2019 installation compliance, AS 4777.2:2020 grid protection, and Clean Energy Council approved-product listing are
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-australia.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Brazil (INMETRO): ABNT NBR IEC 62619 safety testing (required by project specification and ANEEL grid access — confirmed; INMETRO compulsory certification scope for complete stationary BESS not confirmed as of
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-brazil.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Canada (Health Canada): For Canada, the main BESS gaps are SCC-accepted Canadian electrical certification, CSA/UL system and battery safety evidence, provincial fire/building and electrical-code installation approval, CSA C22.3 No. 9 utility
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-canada.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Chile: For BESS exported from China to Chile, there is no single official whole-unit BESS certificate that should be assumed mandatory for every project. The core gap is scope
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-chile
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-chile.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Egypt: For BESS exported from China to Egypt, no blanket whole-unit mandatory BESS certification has been confirmed from the cited public sources. The practical compliance file should instead be
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-egypt
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-egypt.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> European Union (CE): Beyond CE safety, the EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 requirements for carbon footprint, due diligence, and labelling, plus UN 38.3 transport testing, are the major new obligations.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-eu.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> India (BIS/ALMM): CEA grid-connectivity approval from the relevant State Electricity Regulatory Commission is a clearly established mandatory requirement. BIS CRS/FMCS registration under IS/IEC 62619 applies at the CELL and MODULE
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-india.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Indonesia: For BESS exported from China to Indonesia, the safest working assumption is not a single whole-unit certification path. Map the product to adopted SNI battery standards such as
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-indonesia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-indonesia.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Japan (PSE/JET): PSE/DENAN scope uncertainty for stationary BESS systems is the first issue to resolve; Fire Service Act hazardous-materials designation above capacity thresholds, Electricity Business Act technical standards and grid-connection
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-japan.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): For China-made BESS entering Mexico, the practical baseline is NOM electrical safety and installation compliance: NOM-003-SCFI for in-scope electrical products, NOM-001-SEDE for installations, certification through an accredited body
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-mexico.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Nigeria: For BESS exported from China to Nigeria, SONCAP is mandatory where the imported goods are SONCAP-regulated, but a blanket mandatory whole-unit BESS product certificate should not be assumed
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-nigeria
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-nigeria.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Pakistan: For BESS exported from China to Pakistan, do not assume a blanket mandatory whole-unit lithium BESS PSQCA certificate unless the exact HS code, Pakistan Standard, compulsory-item listing, tender,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-pakistan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-pakistan.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Philippines: For BESS exported from China to the Philippines, do not assume a whole-unit lithium BESS BPS mandatory certification route unless a specific BPS technical regulation, importer route, or
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-philippines
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-philippines.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Saudi Arabia (SASO/SABER): IEC 62619 system certification via SABER PCoC, SEC Grid Code compliance, Saudi Civil Defence installation approval, and SASO EMC PCoC are the main gaps beyond Chinese GB battery-safety
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-saudi
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-saudi.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> South Africa: For BESS exported from China to South Africa, do not assume the whole integrated system needs NRCS LOA unless a compulsory specification clearly captures that product or component.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-south-africa
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-south-africa.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> South Korea (KC / KATS): For BESS exported from China to South Korea, KC battery safety certification is compulsory where the product or battery component is in scope, including KC 62619 for industrial
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-south-korea
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-south-korea.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Thailand: For BESS exported from China to Thailand, do not assume a whole-container BESS product certificate is mandatory unless a specific Thai compulsory-standard or project instrument says so. The
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-thailand
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-thailand.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Turkey: For BESS exported from China to Turkey, the practical baseline is CE-aligned product compliance where the equipment falls under Turkish low-voltage, EMC, machinery, radio, RoHS, or related product
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-turkey
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-turkey.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> United Arab Emirates: For BESS exported from China to the UAE, do not assume one blanket mandatory whole-unit ESMA certificate covers every stationary energy-storage project. The defensible starting point is to
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-uae
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-uae.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> United Kingdom (UKCA): A China-made BESS requires UKCA marking under the Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 2016, compliance with BS EN IEC 62619 / 62933, ENA G99 grid-connection assessment, and an ADR-aligned
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-uk.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> United States (UL/FCC): UL 9540 system listing, UL 9540A thermal-runaway test evidence, NFPA 855 installation compliance, and UL 1973 cell or module certification are the main gaps beyond Chinese GB battery-safety
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-us.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Vietnam: For BESS exported from China to Vietnam, no universal whole-unit mandatory BESS certification route is confirmed in this dataset. The main gaps are project or tender evidence for
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-vietnam
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-vietnam.json

## Cosmetics & personal care
- Cosmetics & personal care -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): Chinese cosmetics exporters to Australia must confirm whether each ingredient is an industrial chemical, ensure the Australian importer is registered with AICIS where required, categorise every chemical introduction
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-australia.json
- Cosmetics & personal care -> Canada (Health Canada): Chinese cosmetics exporters can generally sell cosmetics in Canada without pre-market approval, but the product must be safe, comply with the Food and Drugs Act and Cosmetic Regulations,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-canada.json
- Cosmetics & personal care -> European Union (CE): Chinese cosmetics exporters must appoint an EU-established Responsible Person, complete CPNP pre-market notification, commission a CPSR from a qualified EU safety assessor, verify all ingredients against EU Annex
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-eu.json
- Cosmetics & personal care -> United Kingdom (UKCA): Appointing a UK Responsible Person and completing SCPN notification, obtaining a CPSR from a qualified assessor, screening formulations against current UK substance schedules (which are diverging from EU
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-uk.json
- Cosmetics & personal care -> United States (UL/FCC): MoCRA facility registration, product listing, safety substantiation, and adverse event reporting are the core new US obligations; sunscreen reformulation (OTC drug, not cosmetic) and color additive pre-approval under
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-us.json

## Data-center liquid cooling (cold plate / CDU)
- Data-center liquid cooling (cold plate / CDU) -> European Union (CE): Beyond CE (LVD/Machinery/EMC), the gaps are pressurised coolant-loop safety, RoHS/REACH plus F-gas/PFAS coolant rules, and EU server/data-centre Ecodesign (Reg 2019/424) plus Energy Efficiency Directive reporting.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/datacenter-cooling-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/datacenter-cooling-china-to-eu.json
- Data-center liquid cooling (cold plate / CDU) -> United States (UL/FCC): UL/NRTL listing of the cooling unit, NFPA fire/liquid handling, ASHRAE practice, and EPA PFAS coolant scrutiny are the gaps vs Chinese GB.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/datacenter-cooling-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/datacenter-cooling-china-to-us.json

## Electric bicycle (e-bike)
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): For Australia, the critical China-to-AU gap is classification: a road-legal e-bike is generally either a pedalec complying with EN 15194 and ≤250W continuous rated power, or a power-assisted
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-australia.json
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> Canada (Health Canada): Canada is not a CE-style self-declaration market: e-bikes must fit federal import classification and provincial road-use limits, commonly the former power-assisted bicycle benchmark of ≤500W and motor assistance
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-canada.json
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> European Union (CE): Pedelec e-bikes ≤250W/25km/h need CE marking under EN 15194:2017 (Machinery Directive); Chinese motors rated 251–400W trigger full EU type-approval under Regulation (EU) 168/2013 (L1e-A) — the critical export-blocking
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-eu.json
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> United Kingdom (UKCA): Motor power (≤250 W UK vs ≤400 W CN), UKCA marking under BS EN 15194:2017, BS EN 50604-1 battery re-testing, and appointment of a UK Responsible Person are
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-uk.json
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> United States (UL/FCC): UL 2849 electrical-system and UL 2271 battery certification (city-mandatory in NYC under Local Law 39), CPSC 16 CFR 1512 bicycle safety, and federal 15 USC 2085 low-speed e-bike
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-us.json

## Water electrolyzer (green hydrogen, AEM/PEM)
- Water electrolyzer (green hydrogen, AEM/PEM) -> European Union (CE): Beyond CE, the Pressure Equipment Directive (PED 2014/68/EU), ATEX requirements for hydrogen explosive atmospheres, and ISO 22734 are the major gaps.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/electrolyzer-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/electrolyzer-china-to-eu.json
- Water electrolyzer (green hydrogen, AEM/PEM) -> United States (UL/FCC): ASME pressure stamp, NFPA 2 Hydrogen Technologies Code, and NRTL listing are the main gaps.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/electrolyzer-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/electrolyzer-china-to-us.json

## EV charger
- EV charger -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): EESS Level 3 registration, RCM mark, Type 2 / CCS2 connector hardware redesign (GB/T 20234 connectors are incompatible), and fresh AS/NZS 61851.1 and ACMA EMC testing are the
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-australia.json
- EV charger -> Brazil (INMETRO): Hardware connector replacement (GB/T → IEC 62196-2 Type 2 / CCS2), 60 Hz grid re-validation, and Portuguese labelling are confirmed Brazil-entry gaps for China-made EV chargers. A confirmed
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-brazil.json
- EV charger -> Canada (Health Canada): The main Canada gaps for China-origin EV chargers are SCC-accredited electrical approval to Canadian requirements, CSA/UL binational EV charger safety evidence, SAE J1772/CCS1 rather than GB/T 20234 connectors,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-canada.json
- EV charger -> Chile: For EV chargers exported from China to Chile, do not assume that a China GB/T test report or GB/T connector makes the charger ready for Chile. SEC product
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-chile
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-chile.json
- EV charger -> Egypt: For China-made EV chargers entering Egypt, the practical gaps are EOS / IEC standards mapping, GOEIC import and conformity-document handling, GB/T-to-IEC connector redesign, EgyptERA and utility connection review,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-egypt
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-egypt.json
- EV charger -> European Union (CE): IEC 61851, IEC 62196-2 Type 2 connector compatibility, RCD or RDC-DD protection, and national installation rules are the major EU gaps compared with Chinese GB/T EV-charger compliance.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-eu.json
- EV charger -> India (BIS/ALMM): IS 17017 series technical standards (with BIS registration likely required — but verify current QCO/CRS scope for the complete EVSE unit with BIS), Bharat AC-001/DC-001 connector hardware incompatibility
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-india.json
- EV charger -> Indonesia: For China-made EV chargers entering Indonesia, the biggest practical gaps are not a confirmed blanket whole-unit SNI certificate, but SNI/IEC technical alignment, ESDM SPKLU business and operation rules,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-indonesia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-indonesia.json
- EV charger -> Japan (PSE/JET): PSE diamond-mark mandatory third-party certification, physical connector incompatibility (SAE J1772 AC and CHAdeMO DC vs. Chinese GB/T), and Japan's 100/200 V single-phase grid are the major barriers for
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-japan.json
- EV charger -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): For Mexico, the main China-to-market gaps are mandatory NOM electrical safety review under NOM-001-SEDE installation rules and NOM-003-SCFI product safety, certification through an approved body such as NYCE
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-mexico.json
- EV charger -> Nigeria: For China-made EV chargers entering Nigeria, the main practical gaps are SONCAP import conformity where the goods are regulated, proof against applicable NIS or approved IEC equivalents, IEC
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-nigeria
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-nigeria.json
- EV charger -> Pakistan: For EV chargers exported from China to Pakistan, the main gap is not a simple GB/T-to-PS mark substitution. Pakistan public EV charging stations must follow NEPRA's EV charging-station
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-pakistan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-pakistan.json
- EV charger -> Philippines: For China-made EV chargers entering the Philippines, the main practical gaps are DOE EVIDA charging-station obligations, BPS/PNS or IEC 61851 evidence where specified by procurement or authorities, IEC
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-philippines
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-philippines.json
- EV charger -> Saudi Arabia (SASO/SABER): SABER PCoC/SCoC market access, IEC 62196-2 Type 2 connector incompatibility (vs. GB/T 20234), 60 Hz grid frequency (vs. China 50 Hz), and SASO EMC re-testing are the major
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-saudi
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-saudi.json
- EV charger -> South Africa: For China-made EV chargers entering South Africa, the confirmed gaps are South Africa-specific NRCS LOA scope review where the charger or its components fall under compulsory electrotechnical specifications,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-south-africa
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-south-africa.json
- EV charger -> South Korea (KC / KATS): KC electrical safety is compulsory where the EV charger is in scope; Korean deployments also require Korea-market connector compatibility (Type 1/CCS1, not GB/T 20234), KEPCO grid acceptance, and
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-south-korea
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-south-korea.json
- EV charger -> Thailand: For China-made EV chargers entering Thailand, the main gaps are TISI / IEC 61851 technical alignment, connector redesign from GB/T 20234 to the IEC Type 2 / CCS2
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-thailand
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-thailand.json
- EV charger -> Turkey: For China-made EV chargers entering Turkey, the main practical gaps are CE technical-file and declaration work for applicable EU-aligned product legislation, TSE / TS EN IEC 61851 technical
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-turkey
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-turkey.json
- EV charger -> United Arab Emirates: For China-made EV chargers entering the UAE, the confirmed practical gaps are IEC-based safety and EMC evidence, connector redesign from GB/T 20234 to IEC 62196 Type 2 /
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-uae
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-uae.json
- EV charger -> United Kingdom (UKCA): UKCA marking, BS EN 61851 evidence, compliance with the Electric Vehicle (Smart Charge Points) Regulations 2021, and UK Type 2 or CCS connector expectations are the main gaps
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-uk.json
- EV charger -> United States (UL/FCC): UL 2594 listing, SAE J1772 or NACS connector compatibility, and NEC 625 installation requirements are the main gaps compared with Chinese GB/T EV-charger compliance.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-us.json
- EV charger -> Vietnam: For China-made EV chargers entering Vietnam, the main practical gaps are TCVN / IEC 61851 evidence, GB/T 20234-to-Type 2 or CCS2 connector redesign where the project specifies IEC
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-vietnam
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-vietnam.json

## Food-contact materials
- Food-contact materials -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): Australia does not operate an EU-style food-contact material authorisation or positive-list system for all packaging materials. Chinese exporters should instead demonstrate that the material will not make food
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-australia.json
- Food-contact materials -> Canada (Health Canada): Chinese food-contact material exporters targeting Canada must satisfy the Food and Drugs Act and Food and Drug Regulations Division 23 safety rule that packaging must not yield harmful
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-canada.json
- Food-contact materials -> European Union (CE): Chinese food-contact material exporters targeting the EU must satisfy Reg 1935/2004 safety and inertness requirements, check every intentionally added substance against the EU Union list (Reg 10/2011 for
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-eu.json
- Food-contact materials -> United Kingdom (UKCA): UK-retained Regulation 1935/2004, UK-assimilated Plastics Regulation 10/2011 (substance authorisation + migration limits + Annex III testing), GMP Regulation 2023/2006, and a written English-language Declaration of Compliance are the
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-uk.json
- Food-contact materials -> United States (UL/FCC): The US FDA regulates food-contact materials through a substance-by-substance approval system under 21 CFR 174-179 — critically, FDA issues no premarket certificate or compliance mark, so manufacturers must
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-us.json

## Furniture
- Furniture -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): For Chinese furniture exported to Australia, the main gaps are: mandatory ACCC toppling furniture warning and safety-information rules from 4 May 2025 for covered clothing storage units, bookcases,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-australia.json
- Furniture -> Canada (Health Canada): For Chinese furniture exported to Canada, the main gaps are CCPSA general product safety and incident-documentation duties, mandatory mattress cigarette-ignition flammability under SOR/2016-183, composite wood formaldehyde limits and
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-canada.json
- Furniture -> European Union (CE): REACH formaldehyde limits (stricter than Chinese E1), GPSR general safety documentation and EU Responsible Person, SVHC/azo dye restricted substance screening, EUDR deforestation due diligence for wood products, and
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-eu.json
- Furniture -> United Kingdom (UKCA): The Furniture and Furnishings (Fire Safety) Regulations 1988 are the biggest UK-specific gap — they require mandatory cigarette and match resistance testing to BS 5852 plus permanent labelling
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-uk.json
- Furniture -> United States (UL/FCC): For Chinese furniture exported to the US, the four highest-priority compliance items are: (1) EPA TSCA Title VI third-party certification for composite wood panels (mandatory federal law); (2)
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-us.json

## GaN fast charger / power adapter
- GaN fast charger / power adapter -> European Union (CE): CE via LVD (EN IEC 62368-1) plus EMC, plus the Ecodesign external-power-supply efficiency (Code of Conduct / Reg 2019/1782) are the gaps; USB-PD interoperability is expected.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/gan-charger-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/gan-charger-china-to-eu.json
- GaN fast charger / power adapter -> United Kingdom (UKCA): For Great Britain, the core gaps are UKCA or accepted CE marking under the Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 2016 and EMC Regulations 2016, UK RoHS compliance, and a
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/gan-charger-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/gan-charger-china-to-uk.json
- GaN fast charger / power adapter -> United States (UL/FCC): UL 62368-1 NRTL listing plus FCC Part 15 plus DoE Level VI external-power-supply efficiency plus California CEC registration are the gaps vs Chinese GB.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/gan-charger-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/gan-charger-china-to-us.json

## Air-source heat pump
- Air-source heat pump -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): GEMS registration, MEPS and Zoned Energy Rating Label evidence under the applicable air-conditioner determination, RCM marking through an Australian or New Zealand Responsible Supplier, EESS safety evidence to
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-australia.json
- Air-source heat pump -> Canada (Health Canada): NRCan minimum performance and EnerGuide filing, CSA/SCC electrical safety certification to Canadian standards such as CSA C22.2 No. 236 and CSA C22.2 No. 120, federal HFC controls, and
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-canada.json
- Air-source heat pump -> European Union (CE): F-gas certification, Ecodesign SCOP thresholds (EN 14511/EN 14825), Pressure Equipment Directive classification, and CE marking under LVD and EMC directives are the major EU compliance gaps for Chinese
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-eu.json
- Air-source heat pump -> India (BIS/ALMM): For India, the main gaps are BIS market-access checks for AC-type products and components, IS 1391/IS 302 electrical-safety evidence where applicable, BEE star labelling for covered air-conditioner categories,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-india.json
- Air-source heat pump -> Japan (PSE/JET): PSE / DENAN applicability, Top Runner energy efficiency obligations for applicable air conditioners and heat-pump products, fluorocarbon controls under Japan's Fluorocarbons Emission Control Act, and re-testing or documentation
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-japan.json
- Air-source heat pump -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): China-market air-source heat pump files do not transfer automatically to Mexico. The main gaps are NOM-011-ENER or NOM-023-ENER efficiency testing for applicable air-conditioning heat-pump configurations, NOM-003-SCFI and related
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-mexico.json
- Air-source heat pump -> United Kingdom (UKCA): UKCA marking (under retained Ecodesign, EMC, and product safety SIs), F-gas quota compliance under SI 2022/1013, and pressure equipment conformity under SI 2016/1105 are the mandatory gates for
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-uk.json
- Air-source heat pump -> United States (UL/FCC): DOE minimum efficiency standards (HSPF2/SEER2), UL 60335-2-40 NRTL safety listing, and EPA refrigerant SNAP approval are the three mandatory gates for air-source heat pumps entering the US market
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-us.json

## Industrial electric motor
- Industrial electric motor -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): GEMS Registry pre-supply registration (unique to Australia, no CN equivalent) and the RCM mark (combining electrical safety and EMC) are the critical market-access gaps; IE3 MEPS floors are
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-australia.json
- Industrial electric motor -> Brazil (INMETRO): INMETRO compulsory certification and Lei 10.295/2001 premium efficiency (IR3) are the primary gaps; Chinese motors must obtain an INMETRO conformity mark and meet ABNT NBR 17094 safety requirements
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-brazil.json
- Industrial electric motor -> European Union (CE): EU Ecodesign Regulation 2019/1781 minimum efficiency (IE3/IE4) and CE conformity for LVD/EMC are the gaps; many Chinese motors fall below the EU MEPS class.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-eu.json
- Industrial electric motor -> India (BIS/ALMM): IS 12615:2018 is the applicable Indian efficiency standard and BIS ISI mark under FMCS is the certification route for foreign manufacturers IF required. Whether BIS ISI mark certification
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-india.json
- Industrial electric motor -> Japan (PSE/JET): Japan's Top Runner Program mandates IE3-equivalent minimum efficiency for 3-phase induction motors (0.75–375 kW) under the Act on Rationalizing Energy Use; Chinese motors meeting GB 18613-2020 Grade 3
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-japan.json
- Industrial electric motor -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): Mexico is not a CCC carry-over market: three-phase induction motors must be screened against NOM-016-ENER-2016 under CONUEE, mapped to applicable electrical safety NOMs such as NOM-001-SEDE and NOM-003-SCFI,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-mexico.json
- Industrial electric motor -> Saudi Arabia (SASO/SABER): Saudi Arabia requires IE3 minimum efficiency (SASO/SEEC regulation referencing IEC 60034-30-1) and SABER PCoC+SCoC market-access conformity certificates; most Chinese motors meet the IE3 efficiency floor but must obtain
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-saudi
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-saudi.json
- Industrial electric motor -> United Kingdom (UKCA): UK post-Brexit regulations mirror the EU framework: IE3/IE4 MEPS (retained Ecodesign via SI 2021/745), Electrical Equipment Safety Regulations 2016, and UK EMC Regulations 2016 all apply — the
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-uk.json
- Industrial electric motor -> United States (UL/FCC): US DOE energy conservation standards, NEMA Premium or IE3-equivalent efficiency expectations, NEMA MG-1 design evidence, UL listing, and IEEE 112 Method B testing are the main gaps; GB/T
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-us.json

## LED luminaire
- LED luminaire -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering Australia must have an Australian responsible supplier/importer, meet electrical safety obligations under state and territory law and the EESS framework, apply the
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-australia.json
- LED luminaire -> Canada (Health Canada): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering Canada should plan for Canadian electrical safety approval through an SCC-accredited certification body using the CSA C22.2 No. 250 series or the
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-canada.json
- LED luminaire -> European Union (CE): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering the EU market must obtain CE marking under LVD (EN 60598-1), EMC (EN 55015 / EN 61547), and RoHS directives; comply with
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-eu.json
- LED luminaire -> India (BIS/ALMM): A Chinese LED luminaire or LED lamp manufacturer entering India must first confirm whether the exact product is notified under the Electronics and IT Goods (Requirements for Compulsory
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-india.json
- LED luminaire -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering Mexico should plan for Mexican NOM conformity before shipment: screen the luminaire and LED driver against NOM-058-SCFI luminaire safety and NOM-003-SCFI electrical-product
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-mexico.json
- LED luminaire -> United Kingdom (UKCA): LED luminaires exported from China to the UK require UKCA marking (or CE under current transitional easement), compliance with BS EN 60598, ecodesign efficacy thresholds under SI 2021/1095,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-uk.json
- LED luminaire -> United States (UL/FCC): NRTL certification (UL 1598 + UL 8750) and FCC Part 15B SDoC are the critical US gaps for Chinese LED luminaires. Note: there is no single federal consumer
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-us.json

## Lithium battery / power bank
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters must check whether the product contains button/coin cells covered by ACCC mandatory standards, appoint or work through an Australian Responsible Supplier
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-australia.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Canada (Health Canada): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Canada should not rely on CCC, GB 31241, or a CE-style single mark. Canada typically requires TDG/UN 38.3 transport documentation,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-canada.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> European Union (CE): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters must obtain EN IEC 62133-2 testing, CE marking under LVD and Battery Regulation 2023/1542, an EU Authorised Representative, and EPR registration
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-eu.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> India (BIS/ALMM): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to India should treat BIS CRS registration as a separate Indian market-access gate: sealed secondary cells/batteries are covered by IS 16046
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-india.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Mexico should treat NOM compliance as a separate market-access project: in-scope power banks and electronic products may require NOM-001-SCFI safety
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-mexico.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> United Kingdom (UKCA): BS EN IEC 62133-2 cell/pack safety testing, UN 38.3 transport test report, UK Batteries & Accumulators Regulations 2008 registration, UK EPR producer registration, and appointment of a UK
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-uk.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> United States (UL/FCC): To export lithium batteries or power banks from China to the United States, you need: (1) NRTL safety listing under UL 1642 (cells), UL 2054 (packs), or UL
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-us.json

## Home/clinical medical device
- Home/clinical medical device -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): Chinese manufacturers entering Australia must appoint an Australian Sponsor, obtain ARTG inclusion under TGA classification, hold an ISO 13485 certificate from a TGA-recognised CAB, demonstrate AS/NZS IEC 60601-1
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-australia.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Brazil (INMETRO): Brazil requires ANVISA registration or notification (RDC 751/2022 Classes I–IV), Brazilian GMP certification (B-GMP, mandatory for Class III/IV), INMETRO electromedical certification (ABNT NBR IEC 60601-1), a Brazil-incorporated Registration
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-brazil.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Canada (Health Canada): MDSAP-certified ISO 13485 QMS (mandatory for Class II–IV manufacturers since 2019 via MDL), a Canadian MDL, an MDEL for the importer (requiring documented procedures per s.45 — not
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-canada.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> European Union (CE): EU MDR 2017/745 conformity, usually through a Notified Body, an EU Authorised Representative, EUDAMED and UDI obligations, and ISO 13485 QMS evidence are the main gaps beyond NMPA;
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-eu.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> India (BIS/ALMM): Appointing an Indian Authorized Agent and obtaining a CDSCO Import Licence (Form MD-15 via MD-14 application on SUGAM) are the primary structural gaps versus NMPA; IS/IEC 60601-1 safety
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-india.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Japan (PSE/JET): Japan's PMD Act requires a licensed Japanese Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH) — a Chinese manufacturer cannot hold the authorization directly — plus Foreign Manufacturer Registration, MHLW Ordinance 169
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-japan.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): Mexico generally requires COFEPRIS sanitary registration before sale or import of medical devices, local Class I/II/III classification, applicable NOM compliance including NOM-241-SSA1 GMP and Spanish labelling under NOM-137-SSA1,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-mexico.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Saudi Arabia (SASO/SABER): SFDA Medical Device Marketing Authorization (MDMA), a Saudi-based Authorized Representative, ISO 13485 QMS certification by an SFDA-recognized body, and Arabic-language labelling are the primary new requirements beyond NMPA;
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-saudi
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-saudi.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> United Kingdom (UKCA): UKCA marking (or CE under transitional acceptance expiring by class by 2030), a mandatory UK Responsible Person registered on MHRA DORS, and ISO 13485 QMS assessed by a
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-uk.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> United States (UL/FCC): FDA 510(k) clearance, establishment registration and device listing, a US Agent, and 21 CFR 820 QMS are the main gaps beyond NMPA; IEC 60601 safety largely transfers from
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-us.json

## Sodium-ion battery storage (Na-ion)
- Sodium-ion battery storage (Na-ion) -> European Union (CE): Na-ion faces the same EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 obligations as Li-ion, including carbon footprint, due diligence, and battery passport requirements, plus CE and EN IEC 62619 expectations; one
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/na-ion-storage-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/na-ion-storage-china-to-eu.json
- Sodium-ion battery storage (Na-ion) -> United Kingdom (UKCA): Na-ion BESS entering Great Britain must be assessed under the applicable UK product-safety SIs for UKCA or currently recognised CE marking, with BS EN IEC 62619 used only
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/na-ion-storage-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/na-ion-storage-china-to-uk.json
- Sodium-ion battery storage (Na-ion) -> United States (UL/FCC): UL 9540 system listing, UL 1973 cell and battery evidence, and NFPA 855 installation requirements are the main gaps; Na-ion sits in the same UL and NFPA framework
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/na-ion-storage-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/na-ion-storage-china-to-us.json

## Perovskite / tandem PV module
- Perovskite / tandem PV module -> European Union (CE): IEC 61215 and IEC 61730 module qualification, REACH SVHC notification for lead compounds (Article 33), and WEEE producer registration are the key gaps beyond conventional silicon PV modules.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/perovskite-pv-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/perovskite-pv-china-to-eu.json
- Perovskite / tandem PV module -> United States (UL/FCC): UL 61730 listing, fire classification, and lead-leaching scrutiny such as EPA TCLP are the major gaps for perovskite modules.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/perovskite-pv-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/perovskite-pv-china-to-us.json

## Power tool
- Power tool -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): For China-made corded or cordless power tools entering Australia, the mandatory baseline is Australian electrical safety law as applied through EESS/RCM for in-scope equipment, ACMA EMC compliance for
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-australia.json
- Power tool -> Canada (Health Canada): For China-made corded or cordless power tools entering Canada, the practical baseline is Canadian electrical equipment approval through an SCC-accredited certification body, commonly using the CSA C22.2/UL 62841
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-canada.json
- Power tool -> European Union (CE): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering the EU, the mandatory baseline is CE compliance under machinery law, LVD where applicable, EMC, outdoor noise for listed outdoor
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-eu.json
- Power tool -> India (BIS/ALMM): For China-made corded or cordless power tools entering India, the mandatory baseline is BIS certification or CRS registration where the tool, charger, battery, or cell is covered by
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-india.json
- Power tool -> United Kingdom (UKCA): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Great Britain, the mandatory baseline is compliance under the Supply of Machinery (Safety) Regulations 2008, Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 2016
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-uk.json
- Power tool -> United States (UL/FCC): The US has no single federal power-tool mark. For practical market access, Chinese power-tool exporters usually need NRTL listing to UL 62841 or legacy UL 60745 for electric
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-us.json

## PPE / respirator (mask)
- PPE / respirator (mask) -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): For Australian workplace respiratory protection, respirators are expected to be certified to AS/NZS 1716 and used under an appropriate respiratory protection program, with selection, fit, training, maintenance, and
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ppe-respirator-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ppe-respirator-china-to-australia.json
- PPE / respirator (mask) -> Canada (Health Canada): For Canada, a Chinese KN95/GB 2626 report is not enough for occupational respirator claims. Canadian workplace programs commonly require NIOSH-approved respirators under 42 CFR Part 84 and selection/use/care
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ppe-respirator-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ppe-respirator-china-to-canada.json
- PPE / respirator (mask) -> European Union (CE): FFP respirators (EN 149 FFP1/FFP2/FFP3) are Category III PPE under EU Regulation 2016/425, requiring mandatory Notified Body involvement for type-examination (Module B) and production conformity (C2 or D)
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ppe-respirator-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ppe-respirator-china-to-eu.json
- PPE / respirator (mask) -> India (BIS/ALMM): India separates non-medical respirator standards and medical-device mask regulation by intended use. IS 9473 is a BIS respirator standard and should be treated as a voluntary presumption route
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ppe-respirator-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ppe-respirator-china-to-india.json
- PPE / respirator (mask) -> United Kingdom (UKCA): Filtering respirators (FFP2/FFP3) exported from China to the UK must carry UKCA marking — CE marking alone is insufficient since 1 January 2025. All respirators are Category III
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ppe-respirator-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ppe-respirator-china-to-uk.json
- PPE / respirator (mask) -> United States (UL/FCC): Chinese KN95 certification (GB 2626) is NOT equivalent to US NIOSH N95 approval (42 CFR Part 84) — there is no mutual recognition, and COVID-era EUAs permitting KN95
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ppe-respirator-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ppe-respirator-china-to-us.json

## Grid-tied PV inverter
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): AS/NZS 4777.2 grid compliance and Clean Energy Council approved-inverter listing are mandatory; Chinese grid-code compliance is not accepted as a substitute.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-australia.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Brazil (INMETRO): INMETRO certification to ABNT NBR 16149/16150 grid and safety requirements is mandatory; Chinese certificates are not accepted.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-brazil.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Canada (Health Canada): Canada usually requires approval by an SCC-accredited certification body to Canadian electrical safety requirements, ISED EMC compliance for interference-causing or radio-equipped models, bilingual EN/FR markings and instructions, and
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-canada.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Chile: For solar PV inverters exported from China to Chile, the main gap is not a simple China GB/T-to-Chile equivalence. SEC authorization for photovoltaic products and installation declaration routes,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-chile
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-chile.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Egypt: For solar PV inverters exported from China to Egypt, do not assume a China GB/T or IEC report alone is enough. The practical package should confirm EOS/Egyptian Standard
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-egypt
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-egypt.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> European Union (CE): CE marking is necessary but not sufficient: national grid-code certification or registration, such as VDE-AR-N 4105, G99/G98, or CEI 0-21, is often the remaining gap beyond common Chinese
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-eu.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> India (BIS/ALMM): BIS registration to IS 16221/IEC 62109, ALMM listing for government or subsidised projects, and CEA grid connectivity are the gaps.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-india.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Indonesia: For solar PV inverters exported from China to Indonesia, the main gaps are Indonesian SNI / IEC 62109 safety evidence where the product is in a mandatory-SNI scope,
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-indonesia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-indonesia.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Japan (PSE/JET): PSE marking, JET grid-interconnection certification, and 50/60 Hz dual-region support are the gaps; Chinese grid-code compliance is not accepted.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-japan.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): Mexico is not a CE/CCC carry-over market: a China-origin grid-tied PV inverter normally needs NOM electrical-safety coverage through an approved certification body such as NYCE or ANCE where
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-mexico.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Nigeria: For solar PV inverters exported from China to Nigeria, the main gaps are SONCAP Product Certificate / Certificate of Conformity / SONCAP Certificate steps for regulated imports, SON
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-nigeria
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-nigeria.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Pakistan: For solar PV inverters exported from China to Pakistan, do not assume a China GB/T report alone is enough. The practical file should confirm PSQCA scope and any
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-pakistan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-pakistan.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Philippines: For solar PV inverters exported from China to the Philippines, the main gaps are not a single whole-unit BPS mark by default. Exporters should confirm whether the exact
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-philippines
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-philippines.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Saudi Arabia (SASO/SABER): SABER PCoC/SCoC, SASO IEC 62109 evidence, Arabic labelling, and Saudi 60 Hz grid-connection approval are the major gaps beyond common Chinese and CE-oriented inverter files.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-saudi-saso-saber
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-saudi-saso-saber.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> South Africa: For solar PV inverters exported from China to South Africa, the main gaps are determining whether the model needs an NRCS Letter of Authority under the applicable compulsory
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-south-africa
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-south-africa.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> South Korea (KC / KATS): South Korea is not a CE/CCC carry-over market: in-scope grid-tied PV inverters need compulsory KC safety certification and KC marking under the Electrical Appliances and Consumer Products Safety
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-south-korea
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-south-korea.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Thailand: For solar PV inverters exported from China to Thailand, the main compliance gaps are not only factory test reports. Exporters should check whether any TISI / TIS product-standard
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-thailand
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-thailand.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Turkey: For solar PV inverters exported from China to Turkey, the main gaps are CE-aligned conformity for applicable low-voltage, EMC, and radio functions, Turkish adoption or use of EN/IEC
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-turkey
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-turkey.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> United Arab Emirates: For solar PV inverters exported from China to the UAE, the practical gaps are MoIAT/ESMA conformity-route screening, DEWA Shams Dubai equipment eligibility where the inverter will be used
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-uae
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-uae.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> United Kingdom (UKCA): UKCA marking and ENA G98/G99 type-test registration are required for UK market and grid access; EU CE marking alone is not accepted.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-uk.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> United States (UL/FCC): UL 1741 listing, IEEE 1547 interconnection evidence, and utility approval are the major gaps beyond Chinese GB compliance; FCC equipment authorization may also apply to connected models.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-us.json
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Vietnam: For solar PV inverters exported from China to Vietnam, the main gaps are Vietnam TCVN / IEC 62109 safety evidence requested by projects or buyers, MOIT and EVN
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-vietnam
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-vietnam.json

## Refrigerator / cold appliance
- Refrigerator / cold appliance -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): Chinese household refrigerator manufacturers face five major Australia compliance gaps: (1) GEMS MEPS and Energy Rating Label obligations under the Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards Act 2012 and
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/refrigerator-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/refrigerator-china-to-australia.json
- Refrigerator / cold appliance -> Canada (Health Canada): Chinese household refrigerator manufacturers face five major Canada compliance gaps: (1) NRCan Energy Efficiency Regulations, 2016 minimum energy performance requirements and reporting before import or interprovincial shipment; (2)
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/refrigerator-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/refrigerator-china-to-canada.json
- Refrigerator / cold appliance -> European Union (CE): Chinese household refrigerator manufacturers face four major EU compliance gaps: (1) electrical safety re-testing to EN 60335-2-24 under LVD 2014/35/EU — CCC/GB 4706.13 certification is not accepted; (2)
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/refrigerator-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/refrigerator-china-to-eu.json
- Refrigerator / cold appliance -> India (BIS/ALMM): Chinese household refrigerator manufacturers face five major India compliance gaps: (1) BIS market access under the BIS Act framework, including product-family review against refrigerator standards such as IS
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/refrigerator-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/refrigerator-china-to-india.json
- Refrigerator / cold appliance -> Mexico (NOM / COFEPRIS): China-market refrigerator files do not carry over automatically to Mexico. The main gaps are NOM-015-ENER energy-efficiency testing and label alignment, NOM-003-SCFI or NOM-001-SCFI electrical-safety scope review, a Mexico
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/refrigerator-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/refrigerator-china-to-mexico.json
- Refrigerator / cold appliance -> United Kingdom (UKCA): Electrical safety re-testing to BS EN 60335-2-24, a new A-G energy label with GPDB registration, refrigerant GWP verification (R-134a is prohibited), EMC re-testing, UKCA marking, and appointing a
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/refrigerator-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/refrigerator-china-to-uk.json
- Refrigerator / cold appliance -> United States (UL/FCC): DOE minimum energy standards (10 CFR 430), UL electrical safety certification by an NRTL, EPA refrigerant compliance (SNAP / AIM Act), and FTC EnergyGuide labelling are the major
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/refrigerator-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/refrigerator-china-to-us.json

## Textiles & apparel
- Textiles & apparel -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): Australia does not require CE marking for textiles and apparel. The main China-to-Australia gaps are: mandatory permanent care instructions aligned to AS/NZS 1957; children's nightwear fire-hazard category and
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-australia.json
- Textiles & apparel -> Canada (Health Canada): Canada-bound textiles and apparel are not CE-marked and do not use an EU-style harmonised-standard route. The main gaps for Chinese exporters are: (1) mandatory fibre-content and dealer-identity labelling
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-canada.json
- Textiles & apparel -> European Union (CE): EU textiles are NOT CE-marked. The four main compliance gaps for Chinese textile and apparel exporters entering the EU are: (1) REACH Annex XVII chemical restrictions — particularly
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-eu.json
- Textiles & apparel -> United Kingdom (UKCA): The principal compliance gaps for Chinese textiles and apparel entering the UK are: UK REACH Annex XVII azo dye testing to BS EN ISO 14362, mandatory English-language fibre
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-uk.json
- Textiles & apparel -> United States (UL/FCC): FTC fibre-content and care labelling (English, permanent), CPSC flammability testing (16 CFR 1610), mandatory third-party CPC and tracking labels for children's products, and UFLPA forced-labor supply-chain documentation for
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-us.json

## Toys & children’s products
- Toys & children’s products -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): Australia does not use a separate toy conformity mark comparable to the European marking route. The main China-to-Australia gaps are compliance with ACCC mandatory safety standards under Australian
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-australia.json
- Toys & children’s products -> Canada (Health Canada): Canada has no CE-style single toy mark. The main gaps versus China GB 6675 / CCC are CCPSA importer duties, Toys Regulations mechanical and flammability rules, separate Canadian
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-canada.json
- Toys & children’s products -> European Union (CE): EN 71-3 chemical migration (expanded to 19 elements in 2019), REACH substance restrictions, EU Authorised Representative appointment, and multi-language labelling are the major EU gaps compared with Chinese
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-eu.json
- Toys & children’s products -> United Kingdom (UKCA): Chinese toy exporters targeting the UK Great Britain market must comply with the Toys (Safety) Regulations 2011 (SI 2011/1881), which requires testing to UK designated standards (BS EN
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-uk.json
- Toys & children’s products -> United States (UL/FCC): The five major compliance gaps for Chinese toys entering the US market are: mandatory ASTM F963 third-party testing (Chinese GB 6675/CCC does not substitute), CPSIA substrate lead limit
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-us.json

## Wireless / IoT device
- Wireless / IoT device -> Australia (RCM/AS-NZS): The main Australia gaps for a China-made Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, or IoT device are ACMA radiocommunications compliance under the Radiocommunications Equipment (General) Rules 2021, ACMA EMC compliance, telecommunications customer
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-australia.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> Canada (Health Canada): For a China-made wireless or IoT device entering Canada, the hard gaps are ISED certification to applicable RSS standards such as RSS-247, display of the ISED certification number
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-canada.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> European Union (CE): Radio performance (EN 300 328 / EN 301 893), EMC (EN 301 489), electrical safety (EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11), and cybersecurity under RED Art. 3.3(d)-(f) mandatory from 1 August
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-eu.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> India (BIS/ALMM): The main India gaps for a China-origin wireless/IoT device are WPC Equipment Type Approval (ETA) for de-licensed radio modules, WPC/SACFA clearance where licensed spectrum or fixed radio sites
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-india.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> United Kingdom (UKCA): Radio Equipment Regulations 2017 (UKCA mark, BS EN 300 328 / 301 893), the PSTI Act 2022 IoT security baselines (unique passwords, vulnerability disclosure, update period — in
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-uk.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> United States (UL/FCC): FCC Certification via a TCB (Part 15 Subpart C/E for Wi-Fi/Bluetooth), a Supplier's Declaration of Conformity for unintentional radiators (Part 15 Subpart B), FCC ID labelling, designation of
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-us.json

# Attribution: Asaptic Cross-Standard (asaptic.com/standard). Confirm all requirements with the cited official regulator and a qualified professional before relying on them.
